text: Three backends supported. Pick one matches already ship software.

title: Automatic Updates
text: Point app GitHub repo re-export. Everything else follows configuration. enable_auto_updates() writes settings _shinyelectron.yml. Verify time :
code: enable_auto_updates(   "path/to/app",   provider = "github",   owner = "your-username",   repo = "your-app-repo" ) check_auto_update_status("path/to/app")

title: Automatic Updates
text: GitHub Releases default, right choice open-source projects. Tell app look, choose whether prompts user downloading. Build usual. Go repo GitHub. Click Releases, Create new release. Create tag v1.0.0. MyApp-1.0.0.dmg (macOS) MyApp-Setup-1.0.0.exe (Windows) MyApp-1.0.0.AppImage (Linux) Publish release. Note File names must match {productName}-{version}.{ext}. electron-updater uses pattern find artifacts. next launch, version higher installed one triggers update cycle: app checks startup check_on_startup = TRUE. notification tells user update available. installer downloads, silently auto_download = TRUE, otherwise consent. app prompts restart install.
code: enable_auto_updates(   "path/to/app",   provider = "github",   owner = "myorg",   repo = "my-shiny-dashboard",   check_on_startup = TRUE,   auto_download = FALSE   # Prompt user before downloading ) export("path/to/app", destdir = "build")

title: Automatic Updates
text: full updates block _shinyelectron.yml: Three flags drive three stages. check_on_startup asks whether update exists. auto_download fetches . auto_install applies quit. Sensible default: check automatically, let user choose download restart. Security patches regressions benefit aggressive posture:
code: updates:   enabled: true   provider: "github"   check_on_startup: true    # Check when app starts   auto_download: false      # Download without prompting   auto_install: false       # Install on app quit    github:     owner: "your-username"     repo: "your-repo"     private: false          # Set true for private repos updates:   check_on_startup: true   auto_download: false    # Let user decide   auto_install: false     # Let user decide when to restart updates:   check_on_startup: true   auto_download: true     # Download immediately   auto_install: true      # Install on next quit

code: updates:   github:     owner: "my-org"     repo: "private-app"     private: true # macOS/Linux export GH_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx  # Windows set GH_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx

title: Automatic Updates
text: Point bucket enterprise distribution. bucket mirror pattern GitHub Releases produces: per-platform manifests beside installers. AWS credentials come environment.
code: updates:   enabled: true   provider: "s3"   s3:     bucket: "my-app-updates"     region: "us-east-1"     path: "/releases" my-app-updates/ └── releases/     ├── latest.yml           # Update manifest     ├── latest-mac.yml       # macOS manifest     ├── latest-linux.yml     # Linux manifest     ├── MyApp-1.0.0.dmg     ├── MyApp-Setup-1.0.0.exe     └── MyApp-1.0.0.AppImage export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=xxx export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=xxx

title: Automatic Updates
text: Anything can serve static files works. server must deliver: Update manifests (latest.yml, latest-mac.yml, latest-linux.yml). matching binaries. CORS headers assets cross-origin. minimal latest.yml looks like :
code: updates:   enabled: true   provider: "generic"   generic:     url: "https://updates.example.com/releases" version: 1.1.0 files:   - url: MyApp-Setup-1.1.0.exe     sha512: abc123...     size: 75000000 path: MyApp-Setup-1.1.0.exe sha512: abc123... releaseDate: '2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z'

text: Symptom: “Error checking updates.” Confirm machine online. Confirm GitHub repo exists reachable. private repos, confirm GH_TOKEN set. Check firewall rules. title: Code Signing and Distribution
text: Users can click past warnings, friction real. Three reasons sign anyway: Trust. Non-technical users read “unidentified developer” “malware” leave. Enterprise. Many policies block anything unsigned outright. Auto-updates. electron-updater verifies signatures macOS Windows applying update, unsigned builds lose update path entirely. concrete outcomes differ per platform:  Side--side comparison launch behaviour without signing macOS, Windows, Linux. macOS Windows block warn unsigned builds, Linux OS-level enforcement benefits optional GPG signature.

title: Code Signing and Distribution
text: Pass sign = TRUE export() electron-builder rest: sign = TRUE tells electron-builder use whatever credentials finds environment. writes signing configuration package.json, sets macOS identity, enables notarization (configured), points Windows certificate. create certificates. credentials missing, warns hands electron-builder, fails signing step specific error. function argument overrides config file. export(..., sign = TRUE) signs even _shinyelectron.yml says sign: false. can also enable signing purely config: Credentials (certificate password, Apple ID password, etc.) must stay environment variables. Never commit config file. kick full build, verify setup app_check(): catches missing certificates environment variables cheaply, without going full build.
code: export(   appdir = "my-app",   destdir = "build",   sign = TRUE ) signing:   sign: true   mac:     identity: "Developer ID Application: Your Name (TEAMID)"     team_id: "XXXXXXXXXX"     notarize: true app_check("my-app", sign = TRUE)

title: Code Signing and Distribution
text: Cost: $200 $700/year depending validation level. EV certificate builds SmartScreen trust immediately; OV certificate builds reputation several weeks, users still see warnings. need: OV EV code signing certificate commercial CA. Common options: DigiCert Sectigo GlobalSign SSL.com EV certificates ship hardware token (USB dongle HSM) require different signing workflow used CI; plan front. Wiring . : Without signing: unsigned Windows installers trigger SmartScreen every download. workaround short signing build.
code: export CSC_LINK="/path/to/certificate.pfx" export CSC_KEY_PASSWORD="your-certificate-password" export(   appdir = "my-app",   destdir = "build",   platform = "win",   sign = TRUE )

text: Cost: free. Uses GPG key already control. need: GPG key whose private half lives build machine. code: export(   appdir = "path/to/my-r-app",   destdir = "path/to/output",   app_name = "My R App",   app_type = "r-shiny",   runtime_strategy = "container" ) # Minimal R + Shiny image for shinyelectron container strategy # Uses rocker/r2u which provides pre-built binary R packages via apt # Supports both amd64 and arm64 (Apple Silicon) FROM rocker/r2u:24.04  # Install R packages as system packages — pre-compiled, no source compilation # This works on both amd64 and arm64 RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \     r-cran-shiny \     r-cran-jsonlite \     && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*  WORKDIR /app  COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh  EXPOSE 3838  ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"] #!/bin/bash set -e  PORT=${PORT:-3838} HOST=${HOST:-0.0.0.0}  echo "Starting Shiny app on $HOST:$PORT..." exec Rscript --vanilla -e ".libPaths(c('/usr/local/lib/R/site-library', '/usr/lib/R/site-library', '/usr/lib/R/library')); shiny::runApp('/app', port = ${PORT}, host = '${HOST}', launch.browser = FALSE)" export(   appdir = "path/to/my-py-app",   destdir = "path/to/output",   app_name = "My Python App",   app_type = "py-shiny",   runtime_strategy = "container" ) # Minimal Python + Shiny image for shinyelectron container strategy FROM python:3.12-slim  RUN pip install --no-cache-dir shiny  WORKDIR /app  COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh  EXPOSE 3838  ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"] #!/bin/bash set -e  PORT=${PORT:-3838} HOST=${HOST:-0.0.0.0}  # Install dependencies from manifest if present if [ -f /app/dependencies.json ]; then   echo "Installing Python package dependencies..."   python3 -c " import json with open('/app/dependencies.json') as f:     deps = json.load(f) if deps.get('packages'):     import subprocess     pkgs = deps['packages']     index = deps.get('index_urls', ['https://pypi.org/simple'])[0]     subprocess.run(['pip', 'install', '--only-binary', ':all:', '-i', index] + pkgs, check=True) " fi  echo "Starting Shiny app on $HOST:$PORT..." exec python3 -m shiny run --port "$PORT" --host "$HOST" --app-dir /app --no-dev-mode app:app container:   image: "ghcr.io/myorg/myapp"   tag: "v1.2.0"   pull_on_start: true FROM rocker/r2u:24.04 RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \     r-cran-shiny r-cran-sf r-cran-terra libgdal-dev libproj-dev \     && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* WORKDIR /app EXPOSE 3838 CMD ["Rscript", "--vanilla", "-e", \      "shiny::runApp('/app', port=as.integer(Sys.getenv('PORT',3838)), host=Sys.getenv('HOST','0.0.0.0'), launch.browser=FALSE)"] FROM python:3.12-slim RUN pip install --no-cache-dir shiny pandas scikit-learn WORKDIR /app EXPOSE 3838 CMD ["python3", "-m", "shiny", "run", "--port", "3838", \      "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--app-dir", "/app", "--no-dev-mode"]

title: Getting Started with shinyelectron
text: default runtime strategy compiles app WebAssembly. R uses WebR. Python uses Pyodide. Either way, app runs browser, browser runs inside Electron. user installs nothing. Use strategy whenever dependencies allow . shinyelectron detects app language files appdir, usually need point directory. packages compile WebAssembly. , spawn real R Python process behind Electron window picking non-shinylive runtime_strategy.  Three execution modes: Shinylive runs entirely -browser, Native spawns real R Python process, Container runs inside Docker Podman.
code: # R (language autodetected from app.R, shinylive is the default strategy) export(   appdir = "path/to/my-app",       # directory containing app.R   destdir = "my-electron-app",   app_name = "My App" )  # Python (language autodetected from app.py) export(   appdir = "path/to/my-py-app",    # directory containing app.py   destdir = "my-py-electron-app",   app_name = "My App" ) # R Shiny with auto-download (downloads R on first launch) export(   appdir = "path/to/my-app",   destdir = "my-native-app",   runtime_strategy = "auto-download" )  # Python Shiny with a bundled runtime (ships Python inside the app) export(   appdir = "path/to/my-py-app",   destdir = "my-bundled-py-app",   runtime_strategy = "bundled" )

title: Building with GitHub Actions
text: shinyelectron ships ready--run workflow inst/templates/github-actions-build.yml. Copy repo: grab directly GitHub. workflow runs three jobs sequence: build matrix, release step gated tag pushes, summary recap. Edit four variables top workflow. fields projects need change: APP_DIR path Shiny app inside repo. APP_NAME becomes installer’s display name. NODE_VERSION R_VERSION pin toolchain; leave defaults unless reason deviate. matrix spreads installers across six runners. runner starts clean image: CPU RAM allocations come GitHub’s hosted-runner specs, evolve time; check GitHub-hosted runners documentation current numbers. Every runner steps recipe: Checkout repo. Set R r-lib/actions/setup-r. Set Node.js (version NODE_VERSION). Install shinyelectron dependencies. Run shinyelectron::export(). Upload build output run artifact. release job runs matrix trigger tag push, downloading every artifact attaching fresh GitHub Release. Commit push fire workflow main master: Tag version cut release: Tags containing -alpha -beta marked pre-releases automatically. Drop badge README contributors see build state glance:

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: Python Shiny now supported alongside R Shiny. app_type takes "r-shiny" "py-shiny", NULL (default) meaning autodetect files appdir. detect_app_type() resolves app.R, ui.R + server.R, app.py; ambiguous layouts abort pointer multi-app-suites workflow. export(appdir, destdir) arguments now works common case.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: Five strategies, legal languages: shinylive (default, compiles WebAssembly runs -browser), bundled, system, auto-download, container. Bundled embeds portable R Python runtime inside Electron app zero-dependency distribution. Auto-download fetches runtime first launch caches locally. Container runs apps inside Docker Podman full environment isolation. older app_type = "r-shinylive" "py-shinylive" values still accepted deprecation warning class shinyelectron_deprecated_app_type; translate canonical language plus runtime_strategy = "shinylive". Pairing legacy type non-shinylive strategy error. shim removed future release.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: Bundle multiple Shiny apps single Electron shell launcher UI. Configure via _shinyelectron.yml apps array; app entry may carry runtime_strategy, suite can mix (example) one shinylive app bundled R app. Python suites read dependencies single suite-root requirements.txt.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: build.type may omitted; autodetection runs build time. build.runtime_strategy: "shinylive" accepted alongside four strategies. init_config() writes template leaves build.type commented lists five strategies.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: New lifecycle splash screen progressive status updates startup. Backend stderr parsed surfaced splash (package loading, font downloads, server status) users see ’s happening instead frozen screen. Port allocation uses OS-assigned ports via findAvailablePort() prevent collisions multiple apps run simultaneously. Splash preloader gain working knobs _shinyelectron.yml: splash.enabled toggles splash state, splash.duration sets minimum display time transitioning, splash.background preloader.background override lifecycle window background, preloader.style picks loading indicator (spinner, bar, dots).

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: auto_download auto_install rendered R-style TRUE / FALSE generated main.js, invalid JavaScript. Booleans now render mustache sections auto-updater wiring valid.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: Added sign parameter export() build_electron_app(). Supports macOS code signing + notarization Windows Authenticode via environment variables (CSC_LINK, CSC_KEY_PASSWORD, APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD).

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: app_check(): pre-flight validator reports issues without aborting. wizard(): interactive configuration generator _shinyelectron.yml. available_examples() example_app(): browse retrieve bundled demo apps. sitrep_shinyelectron() now checks Python shinylive CLI shiny package availability.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: Fixed fs::dir_copy() creating nested subdirectories Windows introducing copy_dir_contents() consistent cross-platform semantics. Fixed GNU tar (Git Windows) misinterpreting drive letters remote hosts resolving %SystemRoot%\System32\tar.exe (bsdtar) explicitly. Fixed Python download URL: python-build-standalone ships .tar.gz platforms, .zip Windows. Fixed bundled R package installation: packages now installed portable R’s library scrubbed environment (--vanilla, R_LIBS_USER=""). Added post-copy entrypoint validation catch file layout issues build time instead inside running Electron app.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: New vignettes: Runtime Strategies, Multi-App Suites, Code Signing, Container Strategy, Security Considerations. Renamed Advanced Features vignette Customizations rewrote around splash, tray, menu options annotated diagrams. Updated Getting Started, Configuration, Troubleshooting vignettes Python support, runtime strategies, Windows-specific guidance. GitHub Actions vignette now reads env-vars block live inst/templates/github-actions-build.yml, documented values stay sync shipped template.

title: shinyelectron 0.2.0
text: Extracted run_command_safe() helper repeated processx::run() patterns. Extracted killProcessTree() shared JS utils process cleanup. Cached available.packages() bundled R builds avoid redundant CRAN network calls. Fixed event listener leak multi-app mode (removeAllListeners() instead removeAllListeners('status')).

title: shinyelectron 0.1.0
text: Initial release r-shinylive support. Export R Shiny apps standalone Electron desktop applications via WebR. Cross-platform builds macOS, Windows, Linux. Node.js local installation management. Configuration via _shinyelectron.yml. Automatic updates via electron-updater.
